This technique relies on human psychology's desire for consistency. When you subsequently introduce the actual, larger request, they are significantly more likely to agree simply to remain internally consistent with their prior "yes."

**2. The Door-in-the-Face (Contrast Principle)**
This is the inverse of the previous strategy. Start by making an extreme, unreasonable demand that you know the target will firmly reject. Once they refuse, acting disappointed, "concede" by offering your true, smaller objective as a compromise.
